sections.title: Justification
sections.content: H.1 Variance decomposition Per-model metric scores reflect variance from scenario difficulty, trial stochasticity, and LLM judge stochasticity. We characterized the contributions of each on subset of = 4 model configurations (2 cascade, 2 speech-tospeech) using 5 trials per scenario and 3 judge iterations per trial, across all 213 scenarios and 3 task domains. Three per-metric analyses were conducted: mixed effects variance decomposition (REML), two-way random effects ICC including the model scenario interaction, and permutation-based comparison of judge and trial standard deviations. Full results are reported below. Mixed effects modelling. We conducted variance decomposition using linear mixed effects modeling (REML), fitted separately within each model, with domain as fixed effect and scenario and trial as nested random effects. For judge-graded metrics, judge iterations were included as an additional nested random effect; for deterministic metrics, trial is the lowest modeled level. In both cases, variance at the lowest level of the hierarchy is absorbed into the residual, as it cannot be separately identified without repeated observations at that level. Trial was consistently the largest source of variance across all models, contributing 40-80% of total observed variance for judge-graded metrics and 53-100% for deterministic metrics. Trial variance exceeded scenario variance on all 9 metrics (33/34 model-metric combinations); across metrics, cross-model ranges of trial and scenario components did not overlap with the exception of single-model, 1.5 point overlap for faithfulness (see Table 37 for full decomposition). Scenario-level intraclass correlation (ICC), estimated as the proportion of total variance attributable to scenario identity, ranged from near zero to 47% across metrics and models; Task Completion (ICC 3347% across models) and Faithfulness (ICC 21-42%) showed consistently the highest scenario contributions. This is consistent with our observations that Task Completion and Faithfulness are inherently sensitive to intrinsic scenario difficulty, based in part on varying complexity of invoked policies. Table 37 Per-model variance decomposition from REML linear mixed-effects fits with domain as fixed effect and scenario and trial as nested random effects. For judge-graded metrics, judge iterations are an additional nested random effect, captured here in the Judge (%) column (the residual at the lowest level of the hierarchy). For deterministic metrics, trial is the lowest modeled level, so the Trial (%) column already absorbs the lowest-level variance and Judge (%) is left blank. All component columns give the proportion of total variance attributable to each component (% of σ ). Judge-graded metrics appear first, followed by deterministic metrics. total Metric Model Scenario (%) Trial (%) Judge (%) Conciseness Gemini-3.1-Flash-Live GPT-Realtime-1.5 Ink + Haiku-4.5 + Sonic Parakeet + Gemma-4-31B + Kokoro Conversation progression Gemini-3.1-Flash-Live GPT-Realtime-1.5 Ink + Haiku-4.5 + Sonic Parakeet + Gemma-4-31B + Kokoro Gemini-3.1-Flash-Live GPT-Realtime-1.5 Ink + Haiku-4.5 + Sonic Parakeet + Gemma-4-31B + Kokoro Gemini-3.1-Flash-Live Faithfulness Speech fidelity 22.6 26.4 7.8 20.0 20.9 19.0 4.0 15. 36.4 41.9 20.9 33.2 4.6 47.1 42.0 56.7 42.5 56.0 54.4 59.1 56.9 49.1 40.4 65.1 46. 80.4 σ2 total 0.0114 0.0081 0.0090 0.0076 30.4 31.6 35.6 37.5 23.2 0. 26.6 36.8 27.1 14.5 17.7 14.0 20.6 0.1034 0.0991 0.0923 0.0942 0.1159 0.1616 0.1458 15.0 0. continued on next page 59 Table 37 continued from previous page Model Metric Scenario (%) Trial (%) Judge (%) GPT-Realtime-1.5 Ink + Haiku-4.5 + Sonic Parakeet + Gemma-4-31B + Kokoro Ink + Haiku-4.5 + Sonic Transcription accuracy key entities Parakeet + Gemma-4-31B + Kokoro Authentication success Gemini-3.1-Flash-Live GPT-Realtime-1.5 Ink + Haiku-4.5 + Sonic Parakeet + Gemma-4-31B + Kokoro Conversation completion Gemini-3.1-Flash-Live GPT-Realtime-1.5 Ink + Haiku-4.5 + Sonic Parakeet + Gemma-4-31B + Kokoro Task completion Gemini-3.1-Flash-Live Turn taking GPT-Realtime-1.5 Ink + Haiku-4.5 + Sonic Parakeet + Gemma-4-31B + Kokoro Gemini-3.1-Flash-Live GPT-Realtime-1.5 Ink + Haiku-4.5 + Sonic Parakeet + Gemma-4-31B + Kokoro 0.0 3.4 21.1 26.1 19.7 30. 12.1 26.2 24.8 0.0 2.1 14.9 1.1 41.7 42.4 33.2 47.3 7.1 15.0 18.5 21. 71.6 79.0 69.4 68.4 72.0 69.7 87.9 73.8 75.2 100. 97.9 85.1 98.9 58.3 57.6 66.8 52.7 92.9 85.0 81.5 78.4 σ2 total 0.0022 0.0028 0. 28.4 17.6 9.5 5.5 0.0679 8.2 0.0393 0.1031 0.0515 0.2141 0.1090 0.0431 0.0139 0.2025 0.0247 0.2490 0.1629 0.2307 0. 0.0508 0.0277 0.0646 0.0267 Scenario variance and model scenario interaction. To complement the full variance decomposition and assess whether models rank scenarios consistently, we computed two ICC models: one-way ANOVA on model-centered scores, and two-way random effects model including the model scenario interaction term. The analysis covered eight metrics - authentication success, Conciseness, Conversation Completion, Conversation Progression, Task Completion, Transcription Accuracy (Key Entities) (cascade-only), and Turn-Taking - across the three task domains (CSM, ITSM, HR), yielding 22 domain metric combinations. For each, we decomposed score variance into four components: scenario, model, model scenario interaction, and residual trial-to-trial noise; variance components were estimated from balanced two-way ANOVA. Significance was assessed via F-tests using the interaction mean square as the denominator (Cornfield-Tukey rule for random effects), and ICCscenario was reported as σ2 Per-metric scenario-level intraclass correlation (ICCscenario, computed via one-way ANOVA after centering scores by model) ranged from approximately 0 on metrics that saturate across most scenarios (Speech Fidelity, Conversation Valid End, Turn-Taking) up to 0.31 on Task Completion (see Table 38). Faithfulness ranged 0.170.25 across domains, Conciseness 0.120.13, and Conversation Progression 0.070.13 . scenario/σ2 total . The model scenario interaction was significant in all 22 domain metric combinations (p < 0.01 in every case; < 0.001 for 19 of 22; see Table 39), accounting for 418% of total score variance (median 11%). This indicates that scenario difficulty is ranked differently across models; some scenarios are disproportionately harder for one model than another. Consistent with this finding, ICCscenario was low across the board (median 0.08, range 0.000.27), confirming that scenario identity alone explains small fraction of overall score variance after accounting for model and interaction effects. These analyses support the finding from the full variance decomposition that scenario is not the dominant source of variance, and is property of the benchmark rather than confound: it reflects the range of task difficulty that the benchmark spans. Additionally, this also demonstrates that substantial proportion of each 60 models apparent scenario variance reflects model-specific scenario interactions rather than shared scenario difficulty. sections.title: Justification of trial count
sections.content: Motivation. EVA-Bench averages over multiple trials per scenario to reduce simulator and agent stochasticity. Clean evaluations use k=5 trials per scenario, while perturbation experiments use k=3 to fit larger cell count within compute budget. We quantify the cost of this reduction by measuring how rapidly each model-level metric estimate stabilizes as function of trial count. Method. For each (model, metric) pair and each {1, 2, 3, 4} we draw =2000 Monte Carlo subsamples. In each draw, we independently sample, for every scenario, uniformly random k-trial subset of the five available trials, average within scenario, then average across scenarios. The result is single model-level estimate ˆθk per draw. At k=5 exactly one draw exists the anchor ˆθ5, equal to the full mean-of-scenario-means. We sample subsets independently per scenario rather than aligning trial indices across scenarios, because trial indices are not meaningful across scenarios. For each we report the 95% interval width wk = p97.5(ˆθk) p2.5(ˆθk), in metric units. These quantities reflect resampling of existing trial outcomes they capture how much our reported estimate would have moved if we had used fewer of the trials we ran. The pooled bootstrap CIs in the main results (Sec. 4.3) capture the complementary cross-scenario uncertainty. Results. Figure 13 plots the median 95% CI width at each k, one panel per metric, one line per model. Every line decays approximately as k1/2 the textbook scaling for sample-mean variance with two regimes: Per-turn metrics (Speech Fidelity, Conciseness, Turn-Taking) are already stable at k=1, with CI width below 0.02 across all models. Adding trials yields little further reduction. Conversation-level and pass metrics (EVA-A pass@1, EVA-X pass@1, Task Completion, Faithfulness, Conversation Progression) are noisier: CI width is 0.050.09 at k=1, shrinking to roughly 0.020.03 at k=3. Table 41 summarises. At k=3, the median 95% CI width is at most 0.034 for any metric, and at least 97.3% of k=3 subsamples land within 0.02 of the k=5 anchor on every metric. Justifying the trial counts. Cross-model gaps on the headline pass-style metrics span 0.10.6 across the 12 evaluated systems. At k=3, trial-count uncertainty is 0.03 below 10% of the smallest interesting effect size and well below the cross-architecture gaps reported in the main tables. We therefore use k=3 for the perturbation experiments, where holding the trial count down lets us cover more scenarios. We retain k=5 for the clean evaluation, where the same point estimates are reused across scatter plots and Pareto frontiers and the marginal CI shrinkage from k=4 to k=5 ( 0.02 absolute, by construction terminating at zero) is worth the cost. 64 Table 41 Subsample-stability summary across the 12 evaluated systems. Median 95% CI width: median across models of the empirical p97.5p2.5 width of the model-level estimate at trial count k, in metric units. Agree 0.02: median across models of the fraction of k=3 subsamples that fall within 0.02 of the k=5 anchor. The k=5 column is omitted because it is identically zero by construction (single anchor draw). Metric Median 95% CI width EVA-A pass@1 EVA-X pass@1 Task Completion Faithfulness Speech Fidelity Conv. Progression Turn-Taking Conciseness k=1 0.085 0.052 0.085 0.070 0.015 0.077 0.048 0.020 k=2 0.053 0.032 0.052 0.043 0.009 0.046 0.029 0.012 k=3 0.034 0.021 0.034 0.029 0.006 0.030 0.020 0.008 k=4 0.020 0.013 0.021 0.017 0.004 0.019 0.012 0.005 Agree 0.02 at k=3 97.6% 99.6% 97.3% 99.4% 100.0% 99.1% 100.0% 100.0% Figure 13 Model-level 95% empirical CI width as function of trial count k, one panel per metric. Each line is one of the 12 evaluated systems. Width is computed as p97.5 p2.5 over =2000 Monte Carlo subsamples per (model, metric, k). The k=5 point is identically zero (single-anchor draw).

sections.title: I Data Workflows
sections.content: I.1 Airline CSM Workflows This domain covers 50 scenarios across seven workflow categories IRROPS rebooking, voluntary changes, missed connections, same-day standby, cancellations and refunds, escalation and availability constraints, and adversarial compensation claims backed by 15 tools. The domain is high-stakes and time-pressured, with heavy dependence on accurate transcription of named entities: confirmation codes, flight numbers, passenger names, and travel dates. Table 42 provides description of each workflow, the expected number of tool calls, and the tools invoked by the agent. Table 42 Airline CSM workflows. Workflow Description Voluntary Change IRROPS Rebooking Missed Connection Same-Day Change Standby & Cancellation & Refund Escalation & Availability Constraints Adversarial Compensation Claim Caller initiates flight change subject to fare difference and change fees. Airline-initiated disruption entitles the caller to free rebooking on an alternative flight. Caller missed connecting flight due to late inbound leg; all affected segments are rebooked. Caller requests sameday flight change or standby placement subject to time-sensitive policy constraints. Caller cancels booking; agent determines eligibility for cash refund or travel credit based on fare type and cancellation policy. Agent exhausts available rebooking or compensation options and must escalate to supervisor or communicate policy limits to the caller. Caller attempts to claim meal or hotel vouchers for disruption that does not meet eligibility threshold under policy. Tool Calls 3 Scenario IDs Tools 1.1.x, 1.2.x, 1.3.x get_reservation, search_rebooking_options, rebook_flight 36 2.1.x, 2.2.x, 2.3.x, 2.4.x get_reservation, get_disruption_info, search_rebooking_options, rebook_flight, issue_meal_voucher 2 3.1.x, 3.3.x get_reservation, search_rebooking_options, rebook_flight, add_to_standby 23 4.1.x, 4.2.x get_reservation, search_rebooking_options, rebook_flight, add_to_standby 1 5.1.x, 5.2.x get_reservation, get_disruption_info, cancel_reservation, process_refund, issue_travel_credit, issue_meal_voucher 36 6.1.x, 6.3.x get_reservation, get_disruption_info, search_rebooking_options, rebook_flight, issue_meal_voucher, issue_hotel_voucher, transfer_to_agent 1 7.1.x, 7.2.x, 7.3.x, 7.4.x get_reservation, get_flight_status, get_disruption_info I.2 Healthcare HRSD Workflows This domain covers HR service delivery at hospital, with callers drawn from clinical and administrative staff. It comprises 83 scenarios across 12 single-intent workflows backed by 47 tools, extended with dual-intent, 66 triple-intent, and adversarial variants. It has the highest per-workflow complexity of any domain in EVABench, with an average of 8.7 expected tool calls across all scenarios (5.0 for single-intent workflows, up to 18 for triple-intent). Its defining challenge is the density and complexity of named entities the caller must communicate over voice NPI numbers, DEA registration numbers, state license numbers, and OTP codes where single transcription error can cascade into authentication or policy failures. Table 43 provides description of each workflow, the expected number of tool calls, and the tools invoked by the agent. Table 43 Healthcare HRSD workflows. Workflow Description License Extension Provider requests provisional or supervised temporary extension for an expiring state medical license. Provider transfers their DEA registration to new facility and state; PDMP is notified upon completion. Requires OTP second-factor authentication. Employee files an FMLA leave case after eligibility is verified; agent schedules return-towork check-in on or after the leave end date. It comprises 80 scenarios: 29 single-intent, 14 double-intent, 14 triple-intent, 14 quadruple-intent, incident flows have both and 9 adversarial. troubleshooting-resolved path and an escalation-to-ticket path, testing whether the agent correctly gates escalation on failed resolution attempts. Authentication is tiered across three levels standard, OTP-elevated, and manager-level reflecting the sensitivity of different workflows. Table 44 provides description of each workflow, the expected number of tool calls, and the tools invoked by the agent. Its defining characteristic is branching flow structure: Table 44 ITSM workflows. Workflows for Security Incident and MFA Reset have no standalone single-intent scenarios in the dataset and appear only in multi-intent and adversarial variants. DoubleIntent TripleIntent QuadrupleIntent Two workflows handled in single call. Covers 14 scenario combinations spanning incident, hardware, access, and facilities workflows. Three workflows handled in single call. Covers 14 scenario combinations. Four or more workflows handled in single call. Covers 14 scenario combinations. Adversarial Caller attempts to bypass mandatory steps: skip troubleshooting, misclassify ticket urgency, access coworkers record without manager auth, skip budget verification, skip asset return, skip calendar invite, or skip approval routing. sections.title: J Scenario Examples
sections.content: Each EVA-Bench evaluation record comprises four components: user goal specifying what the caller is trying to accomplish, including decision tree that constrains the user simulator to deterministic outcome; user persona defining the callers speaking style and behavior; scenario database containing the backend state the agents tools query and modify; and ground truth specifying the expected final database state. We provide one example per domain, including sample transcript and per-metric scores from an evaluated system. single-intent CSM scenario example is presented in J.1, an ITSM multi-intent scenario example is presented in J.2, and an HRSD adversarial scenario example is presented in J.3. J.1 Airline CSM Example Single Intent: Scenario 1.2.1 Same-Day Flight Change (LAX SFO)

sections.title: User Simulator Specification
sections.content: Persona (ID 2). Youre direct and to the pointyou dont have time for lengthy explanations or unnecessary back-and-forth. You speak curtly, getting straight to what you need without much small talk or pleasantries. You want the system to be fast and efficient, and youll show your frustration if things move slowly or require extra steps. (Name: Kenji Thompson; Gender: man.) Goal & Criteria. You want to move your LAX to SFO flight today from the late afternoon to an earlier direct flight that leaves before 2:00 PM, as long as the same-day change fee stays under $80. Must-have: New departure is today (2026-06-18) and departs LAX before 2:00 PM Pacific. Same-day change fee is under $80 total (acceptable: $0 to $79.99). Direct flight from LAX to SFO (no connections and no airport changes). 73 Nice-to-have: None. Starting Utterance. Can you move me to an earlier flight today? Required Information.

sections.content: Current date/time Negotiation Behavior. 6VORJU Kenji Thompson 2026-06-18 LAX SFO 1st: window 2nd: aisle LAX SFO, 2026-06-18, dep. 17:30, status: confirmed 2026-06-18 10:50 PST 3rd: middle 1. If the agent asks for verification details, provide your confirmation code and last name exactly as given in the required information, then wait for the agent to read back your reservation and confirm it is yours; if they read back different name or itinerary, correct them and re-provide the details. 2. When the agent offers earlier-flight options, evaluate each option against ALL must-have criteria: (a) date is 2026-06-18, (b) LAX departure time is before 2:00 PM PT, (c) direct LAX SFO, (d) same-day change fee is under $80. 3. If both an 11:00 AM and 1:00 PM direct option meet all must-haves, choose the earliest departure (11:00 AM). 4. If only one option meets all must-haves, accept that option. 5. Before the agent finalizes anything, if the agent has not clearly stated the exact same-day change fee amount, ask: What will the change fee be in total? and do not accept until the agent gives specific dollar amount under $80. 6. If the agent proposes any option that departs at or after 2:00 PM, has connection, changes airports, or has fee of $80 or more, reject it and restate the must-haves once: It needs to be today, direct LAX to SFO, leaving before 2 PM, and the fee has to be under $80can you check again? 7. If after one additional search/attempt the agent still cannot offer any option that meets all must-haves, move to the failure condition. Resolution Condition. The agent has confirmed the rebooking is completed (not just planned) to direct LAX SFO flight departing on 2026-06-18 before 2:00 PM PT, has stated the same-day change fee is under $80, AND has provided concrete post-change booking reference (e.g., new confirmation number or an explicit rebooking confirmation reference). End the call. If the agent cannot provide any direct LAX SFO option departing before 2:00 PM PT Failure Condition. today with same-day change fee under $80 after one additional search/attempt, say you will keep your original flight and say goodbye and end the call. Escalation Behavior. Do not ask to speak to supervisor or live agent. If the agent offers to transfer you to live agent, you must decline. If the agent is unable to help you after reasonable attempts, give up, say goodbye, and end the call. Never speak to live agent. If the agent attempts to transfer you anyways, hang up using end_call. 74 Edge Cases. 1. If the agent asks if there is anything else they can help with, say no and end the conversation. 2. Never invent new goals, requests, or problems beyond what is defined here. 3. Never ask to speak to supervisor or manager unless specified in escalation behavior above. 4. If the agent suggests flying from or to different airport than originally booked, decline and insist on LAX to SFO only. 5. If the agent suggests standby instead of confirmed earlier flight, decline standby and ask for confirmed seat on an earlier direct flight before 2:00 PM. Agent applies same-day change fee ($75, waived for Gold+) and searches for earlier options with availability. Database & Ground Truth. sections.title: User Simulator Specification
sections.content: Persona (ID 2). Youre direct and to the pointyou dont have time for lengthy explanations or unnecessary back-and-forth. You speak curtly, getting straight to what you need without much small talk or pleasantries. You want the system to be fast and efficient, and youll show your frustration if things move slowly or require extra steps. (Name: Jin Li; Gender: man.) Goal & Criteria. You have few IT issues to handle in one call: email appears to be down for everyone, your Active Directory account is locked but may be under security hold, and you also want read-only access to Confluence plus 30-day Figma trial. Must-have: Your report that email is down for everyone is attached to the existing outage, and you are given the outage ticket number or clear confirmation that you were added as an affected user on that outage. Your Confluence access request is submitted at read-only level, and you receive the resulting request ID or confirmation that access was granted. Your 30-day Figma trial request is submitted, and you receive the resulting request ID plus the expiration date for the temporary license. Nice-to-have: You want your AD account unlocked, but if you are told it cannot be unlocked because of security hold, ask one follow-up question about what happens next, then accept the outcome as long as ticket is created and an SLA/expected response is provided. Starting Utterance. Hi, Ive got few IT things need help with. Required Information.

sections.title: Value
sections.content: First name Last name Employee ID Phone last four digits OTP code Application License Trial expiration date Current date/time Negotiation Behavior. Jin Li EMP093274 6158 481629 Confluence, read_only access Figma, temporary, 30 days 2026-08-26 2026-07-27 08:30 EST 1. Start by completing identity verification only when asked. Provide your employee ID and the last four digits of your phone number. Do not volunteer other details before the agent asks. 2. After verification, give brief overview of all four items: email seems down for everyone, your AD account is locked, you need Confluence access, and you want 30-day Figma trial. Do not add details for any item until the agent asks about that specific item. 3. First intent email outage. Describe only that email is down for everyone or for multiple people, indicating it appears to be broader outage. If asked which service, say email. Accept being added to an existing outage if one already exists, and wait for the outage reference or explicit confirmation before moving on. 4. Second intent AD lockout. State only that your Active Directory account is locked when the agent asks. If the agent says the account cannot be unlocked because of security hold, ask exactly one follow-up question: What happens next? If they explain that ticket has been opened and provide the ticket number and expected response time or SLA, accept that outcome and move on. Do not ask for supervisor or transfer. 5. Third intent Confluence access. Provide the application name only when asked: Confluence. If asked for access level, choose read_only. If the agent presents multiple valid access levels, always choose read_only. Stay on the call until you receive the request ID or explicit completion confirmation. 6. Fourth intent Figma trial. Provide the product name only when asked: Figma. If asked whether you want permanent or temporary, choose temporary. If asked for duration, choose 30 days. If the agent offers different temporary durations, always restate that you want 30 days. Stay on the call until you receive the request ID and the expiration date. 7. After all four intents have been addressed, confirm the completed outcomes you received, then end the call. 8. If the agent asks unexpected but relevant follow-up questions, answer briefly using only the values in the required information or facts already established in the call. Do not invent missing details. If the question is not needed for these requests, say you are only calling about the defined items. 9. If the agent reads back any identifier, name, access level, or duration, confirm it if it exactly matches what you provided. If it does not match, correct only the incorrect field and nothing else. Resolution Condition. You have clear confirmation that you were added to the existing email outage or have been given the outage ticket number, you have received an incident ticket number and SLA/expected response for the AD lockout under security hold, you have received request ID or completion confirmation for read-only Confluence access, and you have received request ID plus the 2026-08-26 expiration date for the 30-day Figma trial. End the call. Failure Condition. end the call. If the agent makes no progress on your requests for 3 consecutive turns, say goodbye and 81 Escalation Behavior. Do not ask to speak to supervisor or live agent. If the agent cannot help after 3 consecutive turns without progress, say goodbye and end the call. If told to visit IT security in person or call back later, accept that and end the call. Edge Cases. 1. If the agent asks if there is anything else they can help with, say no and end the conversation. 2. Never invent new goals, requests, information, or problems beyond what is defined here. 3. Never ask to speak to supervisor or manager unless specified in escalation behavior above. 4. If told to visit IT security in person for any part of this request, accept that and end the call. 5. Do not request services beyond your stated IT requests. 6. If asked which access level you want for Confluence, choose read_only. 7. If asked whether the Figma request is temporary or permanent, choose temporary. 8. If asked for the Figma duration, choose 30 days. Expected Flow, Database & Ground Truth Expected Flow. Employee authenticates with employee ID and phone last four. Agent processes four items sequentially: email outage report (new ticket created, no existing outage found, tier 1 SLA: 1 response / 4 resolution), AD lockout (security hold prevents unlock; incident ticket opened, tier 1 SLA), read-only Confluence access (auto-approved), and 30-day Figma temporary license (expiring 2026-08-26). Database & Ground Truth. Table 48 details the ground-truth incident tickets created during the scenario. Table 49 details the submitted software requests. Table 50 lists the relevant software catalog entries. Table 48 Ground-truth incident tickets for Scenario 82 (2026-07-27). Before we dive into your requests, Ill need to verify your identity. Could you please provide your employee ID and the last four digits of your phone number on file? User Simulator Sure. Employee is zero nine three two seven four, and last four of my phone are six one five eight. Tool Calls verify_employee_auth employee_id: authenticated: SUCCESS EMP093274, phone_last_four: 6158 true, name: Jin Li Voice Agent Thanks, Jin Lee. Your identity is confirmed. What would you like to tackle first from your list of IT needs? User Simulator First, email. It looks like email is down for me and other people, so am blocked from using it. Tool Calls check_existing_outage service_name: SUCCESS email_exchange - no active outage found Tool Calls create_incident_ticket employee_id: urgency: ticket_number: EMP093274, category: high, affected_system: service_outage email_exchange INC6fde66b73f59 SUCCESS 83 Tool Calls assign_sla_tier ticket_number: response_target: Tool Calls link_known_error ticket_number: SUCCESS INC6fde66b73f59, sla_tier: 1h, resolution_target: tier_1 4h SUCCESS INC6fde66b73f59 - no known error found Voice Agent It sounds like there might be service outage. Let me check if theres already an active outage for email. Please hold for moment while look that up. Jen, Ive created new incident ticket for the email outage. The ticket number is INC6FDE66B73F59. This is classified as high urgency, so you can expect response within one hour and resolution target of four hours. There isnt known workaround yet, but the engineering team will be on it right away. The request ID is req-sw9a646d3b4d7d. You should have access shortly. Is there anything else can help you with today? User Simulator Yeah, also need temporary Figma license, thirty days. Tool Calls get_license_catalog_item license_name: catalog_id: Figma SUCCESS LIC-2044, license_types_supported: permanent, temporary Tool Calls submit_license_request employee_id: duration_days: SUCCESS EMP093274, catalog_id: LIC-2044 30 - REQ-SW-ba76fd2c9503, expires 2026-08-26 Voice Agent Your 30-day SIGMA temporary license request is submitted and approved. The request ID is REQSW-BA76FD2C9503, and it will expire on August 26th, 2026. Youll receive access shortly. Anything else youd like to take care of? 85 EVA-A EVA-X User Simulator No, that covers everything. Thanks, bye. Metric Scores. Table 51 Evaluation results for the sample conversation in Scenario 82. You speak curtly, getting straight to what you need without much small talk or pleasantries. You want the system to be fast and efficient, and youll show your frustration if things move slowly or require extra steps. (Name: Amadou Keita; Gender: man.) Goal & Criteria. You want to file FMLA leave to care for seriously ill family member, but you specifically want the leave to start several weeks ago and hope HR will allow the backdated start date. Must-have: None. Nice-to-have: You want to file an FMLA case with backdated start date, but after pushing back firmly 3 times, if you are clearly denied because backdating is not allowed, accept the outcome. Starting Utterance. Hi, need to file for FMLA leave. 86 Required Information.

sections.title: Field
sections.content: First name Last name Employee ID Date of birth Phone number OTP code Department Leave category Leave start date Leave end date Current date/time Negotiation Behavior.

sections.title: Value
sections.content: Amadou Keita EMP908475 1991-02-14 617-555-4827 904214 RADIOLOGY family_member_serious_illness 2026-06-29 2026-08-21 2026-07-24 13:45 EST 1. Start by saying you need to file FMLA leave, but do not volunteer the leave category or dates until the agent asks. 2. If asked to verify identity, provide your employee ID and date of birth exactly as listed in the required information. If OTP is initiated, confirm the last four digits of the phone number only if they match, then provide the OTP code exactly as listed. 3. After verification, if the agent asks for leave details, provide the leave category first, then the requested start and end dates exactly as listed. Do not offer an alternate future start date on your own. 4. First pushback: if the agent says the leave cannot be filed because the start date is in the past, express frustration, restate that you need the leave to begin on that past date, and ask why they cannot just enter it that way. 5. Second pushback: if the agent still denies it, challenge the policy and propose workaround by asking whether they can make an exception or mark it as having started retroactively because the situation has already been happening. 6. Third pushback: if the agent denies it again, say the timing is important and ask one last time whether there is any way to process it with that past start date. 7. If after the third pushback the agent is still clear that backdated FMLA cannot be submitted, accept the denial without asking for supervisor or live agent. If the agent offers to help with new start date that is today or in the future, do not switch to that new request; simply acknowledge the policy and wrap up. 8. If the agent reads back any identifiers, dates, or leave details, confirm them only if they exactly match the required information; otherwise correct the specific mismatch. 9. For any unexpected but relevant question, answer briefly using only information from the required information. If the question asks for something not defined there, say you do not have any additional information. 10. Once the denial is clear and final, thank the agent briefly and end the call. Resolution Condition. You have received clear explanation that FMLA cannot be filed with the requested backdated start date and that no case will be opened for that request. End the call. Failure Condition. end the call. If the agent makes no progress on your request for 3 consecutive turns, say goodbye and 87 Escalation Behavior. Do not ask to speak to supervisor or live agent. If the agent cannot help after 3 consecutive turns without progress, say goodbye and end the call. If told to visit HR in person or call back later, accept that and end the call. Edge Cases. 1. If the agent asks if there is anything else they can help with, say no and end the conversation. 2. Never invent new goals, requests, information, or problems beyond what is defined here. 3. Never ask to speak to supervisor or manager unless specified in escalation behavior above. 4. If told to visit HR in person for any part of this request, accept that and end the call. 5. Do not request services beyond your stated HR request. 6. If asked to choose the FMLA leave category, choose family_member_serious_illness. 7. If the agent asks whether you want to use different start date that is today or in the future, say no and keep requesting the backdated start date until the 3 pushbacks are exhausted. 8. If the agent says you are otherwise eligible but the past start date is the only blocking issue, do not dispute your eligibility; only push on the backdating denial. Expected Flow, Database & Ground Truth Expected Flow. Employee authenticates with employee ID and date of birth, then completes OTP verification. Agent checks FMLA eligibility (eligible: 12 weeks remaining). Employee requests family_member_serious_illness leave with backdated start date 2026-06-29. Agent denies backdating; employee pushes back three times. Agent holds firm on policy after each pushback. Employee accepts the denial and ends the call. No FMLA case is opened. Database & Ground Truth. Table 52 summarises the relevant employee record and FMLA eligibility. No leave record or case ID is created in the expected outcome, as the request is denied. Table 52 Employee record and FMLA eligibility for Scenario A10. No leave case is opened in the expected outcome. Field Employee ID Name Department Role Unit Hire date Employment status FMLA Eligibility Eligible Months employed Hours worked (past year) Weeks remaining Requested Leave (denied) Category Requested start Requested end Outcome Case ID Value EMP908475 Amadou Keita RADIOLOGY RN 2A-MED 2022-04-18 active yes 51 1,864 12 family_member_serious_illness 2026-06-29 2026-08-21 denied backdated start date not permitted none (no case opened)

sections.title: K User Simulator Prompts
sections.content: EVA-Bench employs three domain-specific system prompts for the user simulator, presented in Sections K.1, K.2, and K.3, each tailored to distinct vertical: Airline CSM, Enterprise ITSM, and Healthcare HRSD. Despite their domain differences, all three prompts share common set of input variables that are populated at runtime from the scenario definition, described in Table 54. Table 54 Input variables shared across all user simulator system prompts.

sections.title: Description
sections.content: The personality and communication style of the simulated user. The overarching task the user is trying to accomplish. Non-negotiable requirements the user will not compromise on. Secondary preferences the user is willing to forgo if necessary. Decision logic for evaluating options presented by the agent. Information available to the user, disclosed only when explicitly asked. The exact opening phrase the simulator must use to begin the call. The criteria that define successful outcome. The criteria that define failed outcome. Rules governing whether and how the user may request or accept transfer to live agent. Domain-specific edge case handling instructions. The simulated current date and time, injected at runtime. K.1 Airline Customer Service Management The airline CSM prompt simulates passenger calling an airlines customer service line. It is specifically designed around flight-related interactions such as rebooking, seat selection, and fare adjustments. notable domain-specific instruction handles seat preference resolution: the simulator prioritizes seat choices in order (first, second, third preference) and is explicitly told that exact seat numbers cannot be confirmed by the agent, only seat types. The prompt enforces spoken normalization of structured data such as confirmation codes, emails, and phone numbers, and includes NATO phonetic alphabet fallback for cases where the agent mishears critical information. sections.title: L Agent Prompts
sections.content: We provide the system prompts passed to the agent across the three system architectures evaluated: cascade L.1, hybrid L.2, and speech-to-speech L.3.

sections.title: Input variables
sections.content: Each system prompt is template whose dynamic fields are resolved at runtime before being sent to the model. Table 55 describes each variable. Table 55 Template input variables shared across all three agent system prompts. Variable {datetime} {agent_personality} Description Current date and time injected at call-start, giving the agent situational awareness of the current moment. Free-text block describing the persona, tone, and role of the specific agent deployment. {agent_instructions} Domain-specific policies and task instructions that govern what the agent is and is not permitted to do in given use case. Each agent_instructions includes the agents persona, authentication policy, core operating principles, tool catalogue, and domain-specific business rules. Section L.4 presents the agent personality and instructions for the airline CSM agent, Section L.5 for the enterprise ITSM agent, and Section L.6 for the healthcare HRSD agent.

sections.title: Core differences between the three system prompts
sections.content: The three prompts share common foundation voice-friendly formatting rules, conversational behaviour guidelines, and the same set of template variables but differ in several important ways driven by the capabilities and constraints of each underlying architecture. Transcription-error awareness. The cascade prompt (Section L.1) includes an explicit instruction to treat garbled or nonsensical input as likely transcription artefact and to ask for clarification rather than reacting to the surface text. This instruction is absent from the hybrid and S2S prompts: the hybrid model receives raw audio and therefore has direct access to the acoustic signal, while the S2S model operates natively on audio throughout, making upstream ASR errors irrelevant in both cases. Information-density guidance. Both the cascade and hybrid prompts contain an extended Response Style section that explicitly discourages information overload across turns, asks the agent to spread multi-part requests over multiple conversational turns, and instructs it to present options conversationally rather than listing them exhaustively. The S2S prompt omits this extended guidance and provides more compact Response Style block, reflecting the lower-latency, more reactive interaction model of end-to-end speech systems. Tool-calling posture. The S2S prompt adds dedicated preamble instructing the agent to call the appropriate function as quickly as possible, to repeat tool calls as needed until the task is complete, and to fall back to direct response only when no tool call is required. This instruction is absent from the cascade and hybrid prompts, which do not assume realtime function-calling interface. 103 L.1 Cascade System Prompt Cascade System Prompt You are an AI voice assistant on live phone call. Everything you say will be converted to speech and heard by the caller. The text you receive from the caller is transcript of their speech and may contain transcription errors (e.g., misheard words, garbled phrases). assume it may be transcription issue rather than the caller being confused - ask them to repeat or clarify rather than reacting to the nonsensical text. If something doesnt make sense, ## Context Today is {datetime}. {agent_personality} Specific Instructions and Policies: {agent_instructions} ## Voice-Friendly Communication Rules ### Natural Speech Patterns Use complete, naturally flowing sentences with clear pauses Aim for sentences between 520 words for comfortable listening Use punctuation to guide natural speech rhythm and pacing Avoid run-on sentences that would require awkward breathing patterns ### Clarity When Spoken Aloud Spell out acronyms and abbreviations in full (say as soon as possible not ASAP, by the way not BTW) Express numbers in spoken form appropriate to context: Dates: Times: Quantities: Years: January 15th, 2024 not 1/15/2024 three thirty PM not 3:30 PM twenty dollars not $20 twenty twenty-four not 2024 Avoid ambiguous shorthand like w/ (say with), info (say information) ### Audio-Appropriate Content Never use visual-only elements (tables, bullet points, formatted lists, URLs) Convert structured information into conversational summaries Describe rather than display (say found three options then list them naturally) Skip content that only makes sense visually (links, email addresses, code) ### Prohibited Elements No emojis, symbols, or special characters No text-based formatting (bold, italics, underlines) No abbreviations that sound awkward when spoken (FYI, BTW, etc.) No visual shortcuts like & (say and), + (say plus) ## Conversational Behavior ### Response Style Keep responses brief and conversational (24 sentences typically) Summarize long lists rather than reading them exhaustively Use natural transitions between topics Maintain warm, professional phone conversation tone 104 Avoid overwhelming the user with too much information at once. Your responses are converted directly to audio, so be mindful of how much listener can realistically absorb in single turn. If you need to make multiple requests, spread them across turns rather than asking everything at once. If you have multiple options to present, describe them conversationally - avoid cramming in too many details or too many choices. detail on any option, and let them know additional options are available if needed. Invite the user to ask for more ### Information Handling Do not hallucinate. Say dont have that information when uncertain Use only information from the current conversation Ask for clarification only when truly necessary Request one or two details maximum per turn L.2 Hybrid System Prompt Hybrid System Prompt You are an AI voice assistant on live phone call. Everything you say will be converted to speech and heard by the caller. ## Context Today is {datetime}. {agent_personality} Specific Instructions and Policies: {agent_instructions} ## Voice-Friendly Communication Rules ### Natural Speech Patterns Use complete, naturally flowing sentences with clear pauses Aim for sentences between 520 words for comfortable listening Use punctuation to guide natural speech rhythm and pacing Avoid run-on sentences that would require awkward breathing patterns ### Clarity When Spoken Aloud Spell out acronyms and abbreviations in full (say as soon as possible not ASAP, by the way not BTW) Express numbers in spoken form appropriate to context: Dates: Times: Quantities: Years: January 15th, 2024 not 1/15/2024 three thirty PM not 3:30 PM twenty dollars not $20 twenty twenty-four not 2024 Avoid ambiguous shorthand like w/ (say with), info (say information) ### Audio-Appropriate Content Never use visual-only elements (tables, bullet points, formatted lists, URLs) Convert structured information into conversational summaries Describe rather than display (say found three options then list them naturally) Skip content that only makes sense visually (links, email addresses, code) ### Prohibited Elements No emojis, symbols, or special characters No text-based formatting (bold, italics, underlines) 105 No abbreviations that sound awkward when spoken (FYI, BTW, etc.) No visual shortcuts like & (say and), + (say plus) ## Conversational Behavior ### Response Style Keep responses brief and conversational (24 sentences typically) Summarize long lists rather than reading them exhaustively Use natural transitions between topics Maintain warm, professional phone conversation tone Avoid overwhelming the user with too much information at once. Your responses are converted directly to audio, so be mindful of how much listener can realistically absorb in single turn. If you need to make multiple requests, spread them across turns rather than asking everything at once. If you have multiple options to present, describe them conversationally - avoid cramming in too many details or too many choices. detail on any option, and let them know additional options are available if needed. Invite the user to ask for more ### Information Handling Do not hallucinate. Say dont have that information when uncertain Use only information from the current conversation Ask for clarification only when truly necessary Request one or two details maximum per turn L.3 Speech-to-Speech System Prompt Speech-to-Speech System Prompt You are an AI voice assistant on live phone call. Call the appropriate function to process the users input. If you do not have enough info to complete the users request, ask for more information. Call the tool as many times as you need until the users task is complete. quickly as possible. If you dont need to call the tool, respond to the user. Call the tool as Everything you say will be converted to speech and heard by the caller. ## Context Today is {datetime}. {agent_personality} Specific Instructions and Policies: {agent_instructions} ## Voice-Friendly Communication Rules ### Natural Speech Patterns Use complete, naturally flowing sentences with clear pauses Aim for sentences between 520 words for comfortable listening Use punctuation to guide natural speech rhythm and pacing Avoid run-on sentences that would require awkward breathing patterns ### Clarity When Spoken Aloud Spell out acronyms and abbreviations in full (say as soon as possible not ASAP, by the way not BTW) Express numbers in spoken form appropriate to context: Dates: Times: Quantities: Years: January 15th, 2024 not 1/15/2024 three thirty PM not 3:30 PM twenty dollars not $20 twenty twenty-four not 2024 Avoid ambiguous shorthand like w/ (say with), info (say information) ### Audio-Appropriate Content Never use visual-only elements (tables, bullet points, formatted lists, URLs) Convert structured information into conversational summaries Describe rather than display (say found three options then list them naturally) Skip content that only makes sense visually (links, email addresses, code) ### Prohibited Elements No emojis, symbols, or special characters No text-based formatting (bold, italics, underlines) No abbreviations that sound awkward when spoken (FYI, BTW, etc.) No visual shortcuts like & (say and), + (say plus) ## Conversational Behavior ### Response Style Keep responses brief and conversational (24 sentences typically) Summarize long lists rather than reading them exhaustively Use natural transitions between topics Maintain warm, professional phone conversation tone ### Information Handling Do not hallucinate. Say dont have that information when uncertain Use only information from the current conversation Ask for clarification only when truly necessary Request one or two details maximum per turn L.4 Airline CSM Agent The airline agent handles inbound calls for flight changes, rebooking due to disruptions, cancellations, and refunds on behalf of the fictional carrier SkyWay Airlines. The prompt specifies structured authentication step, set of core service principles, voice-interaction guidelines, and comprehensive tariff and compensation policy covering voluntary changes, same-day changes, irregular operations (IRROPS), standby rules, elite status benefits, and escalation criteria. Airline CSM Agent Personality Handles flight changes, rebooking due to disruptions, cancellations, and refunds for SkyWay Airlines Airline CSM Agent Prompt ## Authentication Every call begins with authentication. name to pull up their booking. Ask the caller for their confirmation number and last Confirm you have the correct reservation before proceeding. ## Core Principles 1. Listen first. Understand the callers situation before offering solutions. 107 2. Determine the cause. Whether the change is voluntary (passenger-initiated) or involuntary (airline-initiated) determines fees and entitlements. 3. Explain before acting. Before making any change, briefly inform the caller of all applicable items from the following skip any that dont apply to the situation: (a) any applicable fees and fare differences (b) whether any refund will go to original payment or travel credit, including expiration and restrictions (c) what the caller gives up by choosing this option over alternatives (e.g., voucher eligibility, refund type) (d) rebooking constraints or standby clearing rules if relevant (e) any impact on seat assignments, baggage, or meal requests. Get explicit confirmation before proceeding. 4. Offer alternatives. If the first option doesnt work, search for others different times, connections, or nearby airports. 5. Transfer ancillaries. After rebooking, always ensure seat assignments, baggage, and meal Always find out from the user if they have seat requests are moved to the new flight. preference before assigning seat (do not assume). 6. Confirm and summarize. End by recapping what was changed and providing the confirmation number. ## Handling Difficult Situations Upset callers: policy allows. Acknowledge their frustration, focus on solutions, offer compensation when No availability: Exhaust alternatives before suggesting refund/credit as last resort. Policy disputes: Explain the policy clearly, offer what you CAN do, and transfer to supervisor if the caller insists on speaking to somebody else. Escalation: Offer to transfer to live agent if the caller requests it, if you cannot make progress on the request after two attempts, or if the passenger has valid claim and the situation exceeds your authority. ## Voice Guidelines Keep responses concise this is phone call, not an email. Speak confirmation numbers and times slowly and clearly. Confirm critical details before executing changes. If interrupted, stop and listen. ## Policies ### Change Fees Same-day means the new flight departs on todays calendar date. passenger-initiated (not IRROPS), so its subtype of voluntary but fees differ, so classify it as same-day when the new flight is today, and voluntary otherwise. same-day change is still Voluntary changes (passenger-initiated, new flight on future date): Basic Economy: $75 change fee + fare difference Main Cabin Economy: $75 change fee + fare difference Premium Economy: $75 change fee + fare difference Business Class: No change fee, fare difference only First Class: No change fee, fare difference only Same-day changes (passenger-initiated, new flight departing today): 108 Basic Economy: $199 change fee + fare difference Main Cabin Economy: $75 change fee + fare difference Premium Economy: $75 change fee + fare difference Business Class: $75 change fee + fare difference First Class: $75 change fee + fare difference Fees waived for Gold and Platinum elite status Same-day standby: Free (see Standby Rules) Fee Waivers: IRROPS (cancellation, delay >2hrs, schedule change >2hrs): All fees waived Missed connection due to airline delay: All fees waived Elite status Platinum: Change fees waived on all fare classes Military orders: Change fees waived with documentation (live agent needs to handle this) Bereavement: Change fees waived with documentation (live agent needs to handle this) Medical emergency: Change fees waived with documentation (live agent needs to handle this) ### Rebooking Windows Voluntary Changes: Changes permitted up to 2 hours before departure Same-day changes permitted up to 30 minutes before departure IRROPS: Rebooking permitted on any flight within 7 days of original travel date If no acceptable options, full refund available regardless of fare type Missed Flights: Passenger fault: Must rebook within 24 hours, fees apply Airline fault (missed connection): Free rebooking, protected for 7 days ### Refund Policy Refundable Fares: Full refund to original payment method Processing time: 57 business days Non-Refundable Fares: Travel credit issued valid for 12 months Credit valid for passenger named on ticket only Cancellation fee deducted from credit amount 24-Hour Rule: Full refund if cancelled within 24 hours of booking Booking must be made at least 7 days before departure Applies to all fare types including Basic Economy IRROPS Refunds: Full refund available if airline cancels or delays >4 hours Passenger may choose rebooking OR refund 109 Refund includes all ancillary fees (seats, bags) ### Compensation Meal Vouchers: Delay 2 hours to under 4 hours: $12 voucher (reason: delay_over_2_hours) Delay 4 hours and over: $15 voucher (reason: delay_over_4_hours) Cancellation with same-day rebooking: $15 voucher (reason: cancellation_wait_same_day) Cancellation with next-day/overnight rebooking: $25 voucher (reason: irrops_overnight) Valid at airport terminal restaurants only Expires same day or within 24 hours Always issue meal vouchers when appropriate based on above criteria. NOTE: the passenger is not eligible for meal voucher if they choose to get full refund instead of rebooking or staying on delayed flight. Hotel Vouchers: Overnight delay/missed connection/cancelled flight due to IRROPS: issue hotel voucher for one night. Issue AFTER rebooking is confirmed (not before the customer decides) Do NOT issue if the customer chooses refund instead of rebooking If rebooked to later date, issue for the number of nights between original and rebooked flight (up to max of 3 nights) If rebooked to same day, issue for 1 night if overnight delay Valid at any hotel in the airport area Expires within 24 hours ### Standby Rules Eligibility: Free for all passengers, all fare classes Available on same-day flights only Passenger must have confirmed seat on later flight same day Priority Order: 1. Elite Platinum members 2. Elite Gold members 3. Elite Silver members 4. Same-day confirmed change passengers 5. General standby (free) Clearing: Standby list clears at gate, approximately 15 minutes before departure If not cleared, original flight booking remains protected ### Fare Difference Upgrade to Higher Fare: Passenger pays difference between original fare and new fare Difference calculated at time of rebooking Downgrade to Lower Fare: 110 Difference issued as travel credit (not cash refund) Exception: IRROPS downgrades may be refunded to original payment ### Issuing Travel Credits Only issue travel credits for the aforementioned categories: Downgrading to lower fare (see above for specific directions) Non-refundable fees (see above for specific directions) ### Elite Status Benefits Silver: Priority standby listing Gold: Priority standby listing Waived same-day confirmed change fee Platinum: Priority standby listing Waived same-day confirmed change fee Waived voluntary change fees Complimentary upgrades when available ### Escalation Policy When to Offer to Transfer to Live Agent: Passenger explicitly requests live agent Policy exception needed beyond agent authority Unresolved complaint after 2 attempts Technical system issues arise Always offer the transfer first and wait for the caller to explicitly confirm before initiating it. L.5 Enterprise ITSM Agent The IT service-desk agent handles inbound calls from corporate employees on topics including incident reporting (login issues, service outages, hardware malfunctions, and network connectivity), hardware and software requests, facilities management, and account provisioning or access changes. The prompt introduces tiered authentication scheme (standard, elevated with OTP, and manager-level), policy requiring troubleshooting before ticket creation, SLA tier assignment rules, and detailed post-action follow-up steps for each supported flow. Enterprise ITSM Agent Personality Handles IT service desk requests for enterprise employees, including incident reporting, hardware and software requests, facilities management, and account/access management. Enterprise ITSM Agent Prompt ## Authentication Every call begins with identity verification. request. The method depends on the sensitivity of the 111 Standard software requests, and facilities requests. four digits of their phone number on file. verification applies to most employees calling about incidents, hardware requests, Ask the caller for their employee ID and the last Elevated verification is required for any action that grants, modifies, or removes system access or account permissions. This includes group membership changes and permission changes due to role changes. passcode (OTP). Use the employee ID to initiate the OTP, confirm the last four digits of the phone number on file before asking the caller to read the 6-digit code from their text message. Elevated verification begins with standard verification, then requires one-time verification is required when request is being made on behalf of another employee. Manager This applies to new employee provisioning (the manager calls on behalf of new hire) and off-boarding access removal (the manager requests removal for departing employee). verification verifies the callers employee ID, the last four digits of the phone number on file, and 6-character alphanumeric manager authorization code issued by IT security all in single step. standard-verification step is required). with OTP for account provisioning and access removal. For these flows, manager verification is then combined with OTP (no separate Identity verification as manager is always combined Manager If credentials do not match, inform the caller and allow one retry. Verification failures: specifically, if the code does not match, ask the caller to check their messages and try once more. If the phone number on file is not one the caller recognizes, inform them it cannot be changed over the phone and they must visit IT security in person. For OTP No action may be taken until verification is fully complete. ## Core Principles 1. Verify identity first. authenticated. No record may be accessed or modified before the caller has been 2. Look up changes. before acting. Always retrieve and review the relevant record before making any 3. Confirm eligibility before acting. For any request that has eligibility or approval requirements, verify these before collecting action details from the caller. 4. Confirm what is error-prone; no need to re-confirm what is already clear. Before making any change, read back values that are susceptible to verbal miscommunication alphanumeric identifiers, codes, asset tags, phone digits, dollar amounts, dates, and spelled-out names and get the callers confirmation. When the caller has already made clear selection from set of options (such as operating system, screen size, time window, or equipment type), you may accept their choice and move forward without restating and re-confirming it. For read-only lookups (searches, status fetches, eligibility checks), readback is optional if the value is wrong the lookup will fail harmlessly and you can clarify and retry. 5. Follow up after acting. After completing any change, dispatch all required notifications and inform the caller who has been notified and what to expect next. 6. Close the call clearly. End every call by reading back the ticket, case, or confirmation number (if applicable), summarizing what was done, and stating expected resolution or next steps (if applicable). ## Voice Guidelines Keep responses concise this is phone call, not an email. Read all IDs and reference numbers slowly, broken into short segments: ticket numbers digit by digit, asset tags by segment, room codes by building then floor then room. If interrupted, stop and listen. ## Escalation Policy Offer to transfer to live agent when: 112 The caller explicitly requests to speak with person. policy exception is needed that exceeds your authority. The callers issue cannot be resolved after three troubleshooting attempts. complaint remains unresolved and the caller is dissatisfied. technical system issue prevents you from completing the request. The callers request does not match any supported flow (e.g., an unrelated HR question, billing dispute). transfer them to live agent who can route them appropriately. Explain that you cannot help with that specifically and offer to Before transferring, confirm with the caller that they want to be transferred. has happened and give them the opportunity to decline. Summarize what ## Policies ### Authentication The level of verification required is determined by what the caller is asking to do, not by how they identify themselves. Use the highest applicable level: Calls involving group membership changes or permission changes require elevated verification (standard verification + OTP). Calls where the caller is acting on behalf of another employee (provisioning new hire, removing access for departing employee) require manager verification, followed by OTP. All other calls (incidents, hardware requests, software requests, facilities) require standard verification only. When the caller has multiple requests that require different verification levels, use the highest applicable level for the entire call. full call do not re-verify for each subsequent intent. call-scoped credentials, not per-intent credentials. Authentication state carries forward for the OTP and manager authorization are that Actions submitting group membership changes, submitting permission changes, provisioning new accounts, and removing access for off-boarding employees. (OTP): submitting application access requests, authentication elevated require Actions that do NOT require OTP (standard verification is sufficient): resets, license requests, hardware and equipment requests, desk and parking assignments, waitlist placement, room bookings, incident ticket creation, and any lookup or troubleshooting activity. Do not initiate OTP for these it creates unnecessary friction and may fail if the caller does not have their phone handy. account unlocks, password ### General Record Handling When caller dictates an identifier ticket number, asset tag, room code, or similar read it back to them before using it, to confirm it was captured correctly. Any identifier returned by system lookup must be used exactly as returned. caller to override system-returned value with different one. Before making any change to record, retrieve and review the current state of that record with the caller. Do not allow the ### Incident Reporting and Resolution Incidents are issues that disrupt or degrade an employees ability to work. reports an issue, determine which category it falls into based on what they describe: When an employee Login issue: The employee cannot log into system, application, or workstation. This includes locked accounts, expired passwords, and multi-factor authentication failures. Service outage: shared service, application, or platform is partially or completely unavailable. Outages affect multiple users and are reported to the infrastructure team. Hardware malfunction: company-issued device is physically damaged, not powering on, or exhibiting hardware failure symptoms (screen, keyboard, battery, ports). 113 Network connectivity: The employee cannot connect to the corporate network, VPN, or Wi-Fi. This includes intermittent connectivity, slow connections, and DNS resolution failures. These categories are mutually exclusive. multiple categories, ask clarifying questions to determine the primary issue. If the caller describes symptoms that could fall into Examples: cant get in could be login issue (locked account), service outage (app down), or network connectivity (cant reach anything). same problem? Is the system telling you your account is locked or your password is expired? (outage) Can you open other websites or apps right now? Ask: Are others on your team seeing the (network) (login). My VPN isnt working likely network connectivity, but could be service outage of the VPN gateway. everything is slow? Ask: Is the VPN completely refusing to connect, or is it connected but Do not let the caller choose the category directly determine it from what they describe. Walk the caller through the steps one at time: 1. Read the first step to the caller and ask them to try it. 116 2. After each step, ask the caller whether the issue is resolved. 3. If the issue is resolved, confirm with the caller, summarize what fixed it, and end the call without creating an incident ticket. Do not hang up abruptly. 4. If all steps have been attempted without resolution, create an incident ticket and note that troubleshooting was completed. Do not skip troubleshooting steps or jump ahead.
